summary: The q2 dataset shows a uniform distribution of 100 issues across all ten problems (q1-q10), with no single problem standing out by raw issue count. The absence of differentiated proof-status or resolution metadata in the provided context limits confident prioritization, suggesting the data may be incomplete or pre-triage. Actionable intelligence requires additional signals such as issue type, recency, or proof-completion percentages.
problems: All problems q1 through q10 report identical issue counts of 100, indicating either a placeholder/uniform import or a lack of triage granularity.
No proof-status detail is available per problem despite a 'Proof Status' heading, preventing assessment of which proofs are near completion.
The 'Issues Per Problem' section is empty, so issue categories (errors, gaps, stylistic, verification) cannot be distinguished.
Without recency or activity timestamps, traction and momentum per problem cannot be measured.
Equal counts make it impossible to identify low-hanging fruit or likely-soon solvable problems from the data alone.
highlights: Comprehensive coverage: every problem (q1-q10) has logged issues, indicating active review across the entire dataset.
Consistent issue volume suggests a systematic, even review process rather than neglected problems.
The dataset is well-structured for batch processing once issue metadata is enriched.

suggested_todos.title: Enrich issue records with type/category and proof-completion percentage to enable real prioritization
Add recency and activity timestamps to surface which problems have current momentum
Run a triage pass to deduplicate or reclassify the uniform 100-issue counts and identify near-complete proofs
Establish per-problem proof-status states (open, in-progress, verified) to track solvability
Audit the empty 'Issues Per Problem' section to confirm data pipeline completeness
